meta.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept CPBS items for SpeedyCAT (chunk 2 of 10). NOT real MCAT/AAMC content and NOT from any answer key. Every stem and choice is plain text (no HTML/SVG/LaTeX). Answer keys were computed programmatically and each item passed a 4-criteria eval (four distinct choices; clean plain-text formatting; MCAT-appropriate difficulty; exactly one correct choice).

questions.stem: A sample starts with 16 g of a radioisotope whose half-life is 10 days. How much remains after 20 days?
questions.explanation: Number of half-lives = 20/10 = 2; remaining = 16 x (1/2)^2 = 4 g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many valence electrons does a neutral magnesium (Mg) atom have?
questions.explanation: magnesium (Mg) is in a main group whose number of valence electrons is 2.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the approximate molar mass of CO2? (Use H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Na=23, Cl=35.5, S=32, K=39, Ca=40, Mg=24, Fe=56, Ag=108)
questions.explanation: Summing the atomic masses in CO2 gives a molar mass of about 44 g/mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the molarity of a solution with 0.5 mol of solute in 0.5 L of solution?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many grams of water are heated if 4180 J raises the temperature by 10 degrees C? (c = 4.18 J/(g·°C))
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The first law of thermodynamics is a statement of

choices.label: B
choices.text: the impossibility of reaching absolute zero.

questions.explanation: The first law says energy is conserved: ΔU = Q - W.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which atom has the higher first ionization energy, Na or Cl?

choices.label: C
choices.text: Ionization energy shows no periodic trend

questions.explanation: Cl has the higher first ionization energy: same period; ionization energy increases left to right, so Cl is higher.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A surface has coefficient of friction 0.4 and normal force 200 N. What is the friction force?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What happens between two objects carrying like electric charges?

choices.label: A
choices.text: They exert no force on each other.

questions.explanation: Like charges repel and opposite charges attract, by Coulomb's law.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: In a transverse wave, the medium oscillates

choices.label: A
choices.text: perpendicular to the direction of wave travel.

choices.label: B
choices.text: parallel to the direction of wave travel.

choices.label: C
choices.text: in the same direction as the wave and back.

questions.explanation: Transverse waves oscillate perpendicular to propagation; longitudinal waves oscillate parallel.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The bond in HCl has an electronegativity difference of about 0.9. It is best classified as
questions.explanation: An electronegativity difference near 0.9 indicates a polar covalent bond (roughly: 0 nonpolar covalent, up to about 1.7 polar covalent, larger values ionic).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: If 0.1 L of 12 M solution is diluted to 1 L, what is the new concentration?

questions.stem: How much heat is required to melt (fuse) 100 g of water? (latent heat = 334 J/g)
questions.explanation: q = m L = (100)(334) = 33400 J for the phase change (no temperature change).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A system absorbs 1000 J of heat and does 500 J of work on its surroundings. What is the change in its internal energy?
questions.explanation: First law: ΔU = Q - W = 1000 - 500 = 500 J.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The atomic number of an element equals the number of

choices.label: A
choices.text: electrons in the outer shell only.

questions.explanation: Atomic number = number of protons, which defines the element.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Overall, the molecule CO2 is best described as
questions.explanation: CO2 is a nonpolar molecule because its symmetric shape makes the bond dipoles cancel, giving no net dipole.

questions.stem: What is the mass of 3 mol of CH4? (molar mass 16 g/mol)
questions.explanation: mass = moles x molar mass = 3 x 16 = 48 g.

questions.stem: A reaction proceeds in two steps with ΔH1 = -400 kJ and ΔH2 = -300 kJ. If the overall reaction is the sum of these steps, what is the overall ΔH?
questions.explanation: By Hess's law, ΔH_overall = ΔH1 + ΔH2 = -400 + -300 = -700 kJ.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A spring with force constant 200 N/m is stretched 0.4 m. What restoring force does it exert?
questions.explanation: Hooke's law: F = k x = (200)(0.4) = 80 N.

questions.stem: What is the strongest type of intermolecular force present in pure NH3?
questions.explanation: The strongest intermolecular force in NH3 is hydrogen bonding.

questions.stem: How many molecules are in 0.25 mol of a substance? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: molecules = moles x N_A = 0.25 x 6.022 x 10^23 = 1.51 x 10^23.

questions.stem: How many protons are in an atom of oxygen-16?
questions.explanation: The number of protons equals the atomic number; for oxygen-16 that is 8.

questions.stem: A covalent bond is best described as

choices.label: A
choices.text: complete transfer of electrons between atoms.

choices.label: B
choices.text: a bond found only in metals.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the sharing of electron pairs between atoms.

questions.explanation: Covalent bonds involve shared electron pairs, typically between nonmetals.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many moles correspond to 3.01 x 10^23 molecules? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: moles = molecules / N_A = 3.01 x 10^23 / (6.022 x 10^23) = 0.5 mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: At 25 degrees C, a solution has pH = 3. What is its pOH?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the equilibrium constant expression K for the reaction H2 + I2 ⇌ 2 HI?
questions.explanation: K places products over reactants, each concentration raised to its coefficient: K = [HI]^2 / ([H2][I2]).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Convert 25 degrees C to kelvin. (Use K = C + 273.)

questions.stem: What is the mass number of oxygen-16?
questions.explanation: The mass number counts protons plus neutrons; for oxygen-16 it is 16.

questions.stem: Which statement is Newton's first law of motion?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Every mass attracts every other mass gravitationally.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The acceleration of an object is proportional to the net force on it.

choices.label: C
choices.text: For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction.

choices.label: D
choices.text: An object at rest or in uniform motion stays that way unless acted on by a net external force.

questions.explanation: Newton's first law (inertia): with zero net force, velocity is unchanged.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A device draws 5 A at 240 V. What is its power consumption?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A periodic wave has a frequency of 0.5 Hz. What is its period?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to VSEPR theory, what is the molecular geometry of NH3?
questions.explanation: NH3 adopts a trigonal pyramidal geometry based on its bonding and lone-pair electron domains.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For the reaction N2 + 3 H2 → 2 NH3, how many moles of NH3 form from 2 mol of N2 (with the other reactant in excess)?
questions.explanation: Use the mole ratio 2 NH3 : 1 N2: 2 x 2/1 = 4 mol NH3.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Using PV = nRT (R = 0.0821 L·atm/(mol·K)), what is the pressure of 2 mol of gas in 24.6 L at 273 K?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to Le Chatelier's principle, removing a product from a system at equilibrium generally

choices.label: A
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the reactants.

choices.label: B
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the products.

choices.label: C
choices.text: causes no shift in the position of equilibrium.

choices.label: D
choices.text: shifts toward the side with fewer moles of gas.

questions.explanation: The system opposes the change: removing a product from a system at equilibrium shifts the equilibrium toward the products.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Convert 300 K to degrees Celsius. (Use C = K - 273.)

questions.stem: How many electrons are in the ion Mg2+? (atomic number 12)
questions.explanation: Adjust the neutral atom's 12 electrons for the charge: Mg2+ has 10 electrons.

questions.stem: What is the mass percent of O in H2O?
questions.explanation: mass percent = (mass of element / molar mass) x 100 = (16/18) x 100 = 88.9%.

questions.stem: A gas at 5 atm occupies 1 L. At constant temperature, what volume does it occupy at 2.5 atm?
questions.explanation: Boyle's law: P1 V1 = P2 V2, so V2 = (5)(1)/2.5 = 2 L.

questions.stem: An endothermic reaction

questions.stem: The law of conservation of mass implies that in a chemical reaction,

choices.label: A
choices.text: the total mass of products equals the total mass of reactants.

choices.label: B
choices.text: mass is created when new bonds form.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the number of molecules is always conserved.

choices.label: D
choices.text: mass is destroyed when bonds break.

questions.explanation: Atoms are rearranged but neither created nor destroyed, so total mass is conserved.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 4 m/s through a pipe of cross-section 5 cm^2. What is its speed where the cross-section narrows to 2 cm^2?
questions.explanation: Continuity: A1 v1 = A2 v2, so v2 = (5)(4)/2 = 10 m/s.

questions.stem: What net force is required to accelerate a 4 kg object at 0.5 m/s^2?

questions.stem: Two parallel plates 0.1 m apart have a potential difference of 50 V. What is the electric field between them?
